signals.reasoning: Cover the live SOL short immediately. It is now a larger live loser than before, retained none of peak P&L, is still flagged urgent, has no stop, and liquidation distance is only about 0.96%. That is failed-position cleanup, not a short worth defending.
BTC is still holding near the upper end of its recovery and is not breaking down enough to support pressing correlated shorts. Flattening SOL is the priority.
ETH remains structurally firm and the event brake is only a narrow CAUTION notice. It is not a cleaner active trade than removing the bad SOL risk.
TST still has squeeze mechanics underneath, but it remains unstable and too expanded for clean execution. Watch-only.
KNEIRO remains too thin and underdefined in this feed to support a reliable action call during position management.
BOME is off the highs but still too noisy and too extended to trust as either a fresh fade or a clean long.
XPL remains extended after the rebound and is not rolling over enough to justify a short. No action.
MERL still looks like a rebound continuation name, but it is already stretched and not cleaner than simply fixing the live SOL risk.
ORDI is fading slightly from rebound highs, but not in a decisive enough way to trust as a short while the broader board stays mixed.
LDO remains neutral-to-firm and does not offer a better active trade than flattening the failed SOL short.
WCT is still inside a recovery structure that looks too messy to buy and not weak enough to short confidently. Stay out.
WIF remains a recovery coin, but the move is already mature enough that a new entry is low quality in either direction.
CHILLGUY is fading from rebound highs, but the setup is still too mixed and thin for a reliable position-management upgrade.
AR is holding up and not offering a clear reversal or breakout edge right now. Watch-only.
AAVE is one of the stronger underlying trends on the sheet, but it is already extended and not a better use of attention than closing the bad SOL short.
RSR is recovering, but the setup is too thin and too underdeveloped to prioritize during live risk cleanup.
VVV still looks like a rebound continuation rather than a clean new entry. No action.
EIGEN is still a lower-quality recovery with positive funding and no clean fresh trigger. Watch-only.
PNUT is already well off the lows and no longer offers a clean early-entry profile. Stay neutral.
MORPHO remains one of the steadier underlying recoveries, but still not enough to outweigh the need to remove the live SOL risk first.
